title: On the (909,228,57) Difference-Set Problem in C_3 x C_303
lean_gate_reason: Lean would currently seal only a citation-conditional statement. Verify still needs an explicit Gordon-Schmidt source anchor showing that 19 is accepted as the multiplier input for the exact [3,303] row before formalization of the intended theorem makes sense.
micro_paper_assessment: Still a viable micro-paper target: the packet contains a real exact-row theorem slice with a structural proof strategy and no bounded rediscovery hit, but it is not PAPER_READY until verify pins an explicit unconditional source anchor for the 19-multiplier input.
family_affinity: Residual-row nonexistence via multiplier orbit compression on C_3^2 x C_p.
generalization_signal: Moderate: the affine-multiplier plus quotient-energy template may extend to nearby p^2 x q rows with the same orbit geometry.
strongest_honest_claim: Conditional exact-row theorem: if the Gordon-Schmidt Table 2 / MC-primes convention indeed certifies 19 as a known multiplier for the exact [3,303] row, then the affine-orbit and quotient-energy argument rules out a (909,228,57)-difference set in C_3 x C_303.
paper_title_hint: On the Nonexistence of a (909,228,57) Difference Set in C_3 x C_303
candidate_theorem_slice: Exact-row nonexistence slice: use the 19-multiplier affine action on C_3^2 x C_101 and quotient square-sum identities to obstruct a (909,228,57)-difference set in C_3 x C_303.
